steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The pronoun before "shores" must refer to "the North Sea." That antecedent is singular and inanimate, and the word before "shores" must be possessive.

steps.title: Choose the possessive form
steps.content: A and D are contractions, not possessives, and C is plural. The sentence needs the singular possessive form that means the shores of the North Sea. The answer is B.
